sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Cal Water receives regulatory approval to acquire Palm Mutual
feed.summary: California Water Service (Cal Water) has received approval from the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) to acquire Palm Mutual Water Company. Palm Mutual, serving 63 residential customers, is located near Cal Water’s Northeast Bakersfield Treatment Plant and currently receives water from Cal Water. The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, with Cal Water planning to integrate Palm Mutual's customers into its Bakersfield District and upgrade the system's infrastructure.

feed.title: Heico Corporation (HEI) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Heico Corporation (HEI) is projected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended October 2025. While analysts have slightly revised their EPS estimates higher, the company's Zacks Earnings ESP of -1.30% combined with a Zacks Rank #3 makes it difficult to definitively predict an earnings surprise. Investors should consider these factors along with other market dynamics ahead of the December 18 earnings release.

feed.title: Down 5.3% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why CenterPoint (CNP) Looks Ripe for a Turnaround
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) has experienced a 5.3% decline over the past four weeks, placing the stock in oversold territory according to its Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 28.96. This technical indicator, coupled with a strong consensus among Wall Street analysts for improved earnings and a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), suggests CNP is poised for a potential rebound. Investors may find this an opportune time to consider entry for a turnaround play.

feed.title: Katharine Weymouth Purchases Shares of Republic Services Inc
feed.summary: Katharine Weymouth, a Director at Republic Services Inc (RSG), recently purchased 478 shares, increasing her total holdings to 5,740 shares. This transaction occurred on December 9, 2025, when the stock was trading at $209.46. Republic Services Inc is currently considered fairly valued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.99.

feed.title: Wizards of the Coast (NASDAQ: HAS) reveals WARLOCK, a new dark fantasy Dungeons & Dragons game for 2027
feed.summary: Wizards of the Coast, a Hasbro subsidiary, has announced "WARLOCK: DUNGEONS & DRAGONS," a new dark fantasy action-adventure game developed by Invoke Studios. Slated for a 2027 launch, the game features player character Kaatri, portrayed by Tricia Helfer, wielding forbidden magic in an open world. A gameplay reveal is anticipated for summer 2026.

feed.title: Bit Digital’s Financial Ascend: Is It Time to Dive In?
feed.summary: Bit Digital Inc. has reported a significant financial turnaround in Q3, with a 33% revenue jump and a shift from losses to net income, primarily driven by its Ethereum staking activities. Despite a revised price target from analysts due to slower data center growth, the company maintains a "Buy" rating and has seen its digital assets increase substantially. Investors are encouraged to consider Bit Digital's strategic moves, especially in light of evolving cryptocurrency regulations and its strong position in the Ethereum space.

feed.title: Verizon (VZ) Edges Lower in Morgan Stanley’s Target Reset Amid Competitive Priorities
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Benjamin Swinburne reduced the price target for Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) to $47 from $48, maintaining an Equal Weight rating due to the company's market share defense strategy in a competitive wireless market. Despite industry-leading wireless revenue growth and increased broadband connections in Q3 2025, Verizon faces challenges from high debt and competition. The company continues to reward investors with a consistent quarterly dividend, marking 19 consecutive years of growing dividends.

feed.title: Raymond James boosts financial reach with $692M Bank Midwest partnership, expanding Midwest wealth management operations significantly
feed.summary: Raymond James's Financial Institutions Division (FID) has partnered with Bank Midwest, bringing approximately $692 million in client assets under management to Raymond James and expanding its wealth management services in the Midwest. The 14-person Bank Midwest team will transition and rebrand as Midwest Wealth Group, offering enhanced investment tools, research, and advisory services to Bank Midwest customers. This strategic move strengthens Raymond James's presence in the region and provides Bank Midwest with advanced technological platforms and expertise.

feed.title: Reduced Earnings Outlook Amid Policy Turmoil Could Be A Game Changer For Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM)
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) recently lowered its full-year earnings guidance due to trade uncertainty with China and unclear biofuel policies, despite exceeding quarterly earnings. This guidance cut highlights the company's sensitivity to external policy and trade conditions, particularly in its global agricultural and biofuels operations. Investors should consider how these external factors reshape ADM’s profitability and investment narrative, although its balance sheet remains strong.

feed.title: Badger Meter announces executive appointments and updates severance agreements
feed.summary: Badger Meter, Inc. (NYSE:BMI) has announced several executive appointments and changes to employment agreements, effective January 1, 2026. Robert A. Wrocklage will become EVP – North America Municipal Utility, Daniel R. Weltzien will be promoted to VP – CFO and Treasurer, Kimberly K. Stoll will become VP – Customer Support and General Manager - SmartCover, and Christina M. Tarantino will be VP – Controller. Additionally, the company updated Key Executive Employment Agreements outlining severance benefits in the event of a change-in-control.

feed.title: Jefferies Raises Price Target for Yelp (YELP) to $32, Maintains Hold Rating | YELP Stock News
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Yelp (YELP) to $32 from $31, maintaining a Hold rating, reflecting a 3.23% rise. This adjustment is based on ongoing analyst evaluations of Yelp's business performance and future potential in the digital services market. Wall Street analysts currently forecast an average target price of $33.81 for YELP, implying an upside of 13.07% from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $45.03.

feed.title: Charles Schwab partners with Texas A&M to establish new clinic
feed.summary: Charles Schwab, through its Schwab Advisor Services and Charles Schwab Foundation, has provided a gift to Texas A&M University to establish the Aggie Financial Planning Clinic. This clinic, part of the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences, will offer learning opportunities for students in the Financial Planning Program and expand access to pro-bono financial services.

feed.title: ITC Infotech wins PTC Partner Network Award for FY25 in the PLM Category
feed.summary: ITC Infotech received the PTC Partner Network Award for FY25 in the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) category, recognizing its strong PLM expertise, commitment to customer value, and strategic collaboration with PTC. The award highlights ITC Infotech's leadership in PLM-led digital transformation and its efforts to help enterprises modernize product development through SaaS-led offerings and intelligent solutions. This global recognition affirms ITC Infotech's deep capabilities and strategic alignment with PTC's vision for an Intelligent Product Lifecycle.

feed.title: NextEra leans into ‘bring your own generation’ as data centers chase reliable power
feed.summary: NextEra Energy is promoting a "bring your own generation" model for large data centers, where customers fund their own power assets while NextEra handles construction and operations. Notably, Google is funding the restart of the Duane Arnold nuclear facility to power a data center, with operations expected by early 2029. NextEra aims to develop 15 gigawatts of generation for data center hubs by 2035, relying heavily on gas-fired generation as a "bridge power" solution alongside renewables and storage.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) Stock Price Up 2.3% - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) saw its stock price rise by 2.3% to $317.30 on higher-than-average volume, backed by positive analyst adjustments and strong quarterly earnings. The company reported an EPS of $5.07 against an expected $4.83 and revenue of $47.12 billion, surpassing estimates. Despite some analysts maintaining a "Hold" rating, several firms increased their price targets for JPM.

feed.title: Money20/20 US: Vegas spins the stablecoin story forward
feed.summary: The Money20/20 conference in Las Vegas revealed a significant industry shift towards optimism in fintech, driven by favorable stablecoin regulations and advancements in generative AI. Key themes included the commercialization of stablecoins and agentic AI, with major players and startups showcasing new initiatives and strategic collaborations in payments. The article highlights the convergence of traditional and digital finance, alongside a strong emphasis on trust, security, and payment flexibility through new partnerships and offerings.

feed.title: Costco delivers strong first quarter, fueled by membership growth and E-commerce surge
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. reported robust growth for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, with net sales increasing 8.2% to $65.98 billion and net income reaching $2.0 billion. This strong performance was driven by a 6.4% rise in comparable sales, a 20.5% surge in online sales, and a 13.7% increase in membership fee income, exceeding analyst expectations across the board. Despite these positive results, the company's shares experienced a slight dip in after-hours trading.

feed.title: Biogen (BIIB) Is Down 5.2% After HSBC Downgrade Amid Mixed Drug Pipeline Signals – Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: Biogen's stock fell 5.2% following an HSBC downgrade, which cited concerns about declining CD20 collaboration royalties and growth risks, despite recent positive news regarding zorevunersen in Dravet syndrome and LEQEMBI for Alzheimer’s disease. The investment narrative for Biogen hinges on new neurology launches offsetting pressure on legacy revenue streams, with HSBC's downgrade underscoring the critical importance of LEQEMBI's commercial execution and payer uptake. Investors are advised to consider the interplay between Biogen's pipeline advancements and the weakening royalty streams.

feed.title: Movistar Arena Santiago, South America’s #1 Arena, Enters Majority-Stake Partnership with Live Nation
feed.summary: Live Nation Entertainment has announced a majority-stake partnership with BeLive Entertainment Group in Movistar Arena Santiago, South America’s top arena. This collaboration aims to enhance the fan experience, improve production capabilities, and expand opportunities for both global and local artists at the 15,000-capacity venue, which hosts over 170 shows annually. The partnership strengthens Santiago's position as a vital touring hub in Latin America, while maintaining the arena's local identity.

feed.title: RXO Named to 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for 4PL
feed.summary: RXO has been recognized as a Leader in the inaugural 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Fourth-Party Logistics (4PL) for its strong Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company's 4PL solutions, which are highly customizable and supported by proprietary technology, help businesses improve supply chain efficiency. This acknowledgment highlights RXO's commitment to innovation and comprehensive service offerings in the logistics industry.

feed.title: Fiserv (NASDAQ:FISV) Trading 2.4% Higher - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Fiserv (NASDAQ:FISV) stock increased by 2.4% during mid-day trading, with significantly higher trading volume than average, while still sitting below its 50 and 200-day moving averages. Analyst sentiment is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $121.08, though some recent downgrades and price target trims have occurred. Notably, insiders, including the CFO, have recently purchased a substantial number of shares, signaling confidence amid solid fundamentals with a P/E of 10.53 and a market cap of $37.05 billion.

feed.title: Data & Dimensions
feed.summary: S&P Global Market Intelligence announces a new podcast series called "Data & Dimensions." This podcast will explore the metaverse's evolution into immersive 3D virtual spaces, focusing on how video game technology shapes the internet's next phase. It will delve into the implications of this digital frontier for industry, enterprises, consumers, and society.

feed.title: Wellington Management Group LLP Expands Stake in Coterra Energy Inc
feed.summary: Wellington Management Group LLP significantly increased its stake in Coterra Energy Inc. by acquiring 5,469,280 shares, bringing its total holdings to over 80 million shares. This move reflects Wellington's confidence in the energy sector and Coterra Energy's potential, as the company is currently valued as modestly undervalued with strong growth and profitability ranks. Other notable investors, including Arnold Van Den Berg and Ken Fisher, also hold significant positions in Coterra Energy.

feed.title: Culp, Inc. Reports Operational & Financial Results for Second Fiscal Quarter
feed.summary: Culp, Inc. reported its second fiscal quarter results, showing sequential net sales improvement and a reduction in operating loss compared to the prior year. The company is undergoing a multi-phased transformation to optimize its cost structure and global platform for bedding and upholstery products, aiming for over $20 million in annualized cost savings by fiscal 2027. Despite challenging market conditions, particularly in upholstery, Culp's restructured bedding platform has significantly improved gross profitability and driven consolidated operating results.

feed.title: Thomas Dean Fountain Sells 1,110 Shares of F5 (NASDAQ:FFIV) Stock
feed.summary: Thomas Dean Fountain, EVP of F5, recently sold 1,110 shares of F5 (NASDAQ:FFIV) stock on December 8th for over $276,000, reducing his holdings to 18,053 shares. This sale is part of a series of recent insider transactions. F5 reported strong quarterly earnings, surpassing analyst estimates, and has issued positive FY2026 guidance, though its stock currently trades below the average analyst price target.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Kristian Talvitie Sells 50,291 Shares of PTC Inc (PTC)
feed.summary: Kristian Talvitie, Executive Vice President and CFO of PTC Inc, sold 50,291 shares of the company on December 9, 2025, reducing his holdings to 12,386 shares. Over the past year, Talvitie has sold a total of 54,949 shares, with no insider buys recorded for PTC Inc in the same period. The stock, trading at $173.14, is considered modestly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.84.

feed.title: Lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald to step down amid languishing sales in the US
feed.summary: Lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald is stepping down effective January 31, 2026, as the company faces a year of disappointing sales, particularly in the U.S. Marti Morfitt will serve as executive chair, and Meghan Frank and André Maestrini will be interim co-CEOs while a successor is sought. This leadership change comes amidst challenges like a weakened athleisure market, increased competition, and criticism from the company's founder.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Satish Dhanasekaran Sells Shares of Keysight Technologies Inc (KEYS)
feed.summary: Satish Dhanasekaran, President and CEO of Keysight Technologies Inc, sold 4,230 shares of the company on December 9, 2025, reducing his total ownership to 125,731.578 shares. The company's stock, trading at $211.43, is considered modestly overvalued based on its GF Value, with a price-to-earnings ratio of 43.79, higher than both the industry median and the company's historical median. This transaction is part of a trend of 17 insider sells against only one buy over the past year for Keysight Technologies.

feed.title: BMO Earnings Call: Strong Growth Amid Economic Challenges
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings with adjusted EPS of $3.28 and a record net income of $9.2 billion for the year, demonstrating robust financial health and effective capital management, including increased return on equity and shareholder returns. Despite these achievements, concerns were noted regarding the softness in the Canadian economy, rising credit card delinquencies, and challenges impacting loan growth. BMO is focusing on a digital-first, AI-powered strategy to drive operational efficiency and enhance client services moving forward.

feed.title: Badger Meter announces executive leadership changes effective Jan 2026
feed.summary: Badger Meter (NYSE:BMI) announced executive leadership changes effective January 1, 2026, promoting Robert A. Wrocklage to EVP - North America Municipal Utility and Daniel R. Weltzien to VP - CFO and Treasurer. These internal promotions are part of a strategic realignment to support growth initiatives, reaffirming the company's five-year outlook for high-single-digit organic revenue growth and maintaining its strong financial health and dividend track record.

feed.title: Texas Instruments, Intel Corporation microchips in Russian missiles, lawsuits say
feed.summary: Several lawsuits accuse Texas Instruments, Intel Corporation, Advanced Micro Devices, and Mouser Electronics of supplying microchips found in Russian missiles used in attacks against Ukrainian civilians. The lawsuits allege these companies knowingly or unknowingly contributed to wrongful deaths by allowing their products to be used in Russian and Iranian military weapons, despite U.S. export laws. The accused companies generally deny direct involvement with the Russian military, stating their compliance with export laws and cessation of business in Russia.

feed.title: Oracle's lease commitments jump by almost 150% as company builds out to meet AI demand
feed.summary: Oracle is significantly increasing its capital expenditures and lease commitments to meet the surging demand for cloud infrastructure driven by artificial intelligence. The company reported $248 billion in lease commitments as of November 30, a 148% increase from August, with $10 billion allocated specifically for cloud capacity. This expansion includes partnerships with major AI players like OpenAI, as Oracle aims to compete with industry giants like Amazon and Microsoft in providing critical computing power.

feed.title: First Horizon Recognized as a 2025 Forbes Best-In-State Employer for Tennessee
feed.summary: First Horizon Corporation has been named a 2025 Best-in-State Employer for Tennessee by Forbes and Statista Inc. This recognition is based on an independent survey of over 160,000 employees and highlights First Horizon's strong company culture and investment in its workforce. The company, headquartered in Memphis, TN, and operating in 12 states, recently reported $83.2 billion in assets.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices' Latest Meltdown Heightens Speculative Fervor For Direxion's AMUU, AMDD ETFs
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is experiencing high volatility, leading to increased speculative interest in Direxion's leveraged and inverse ETFs, AMUU and AMDD. While some analysts are optimistic about AMD's AI market share and valuation, recent significant declines in its stock price and large sell-offs by Ark Invest have raised concerns. The article details how these ETFs allow traders to speculate on AMD's price movements, emphasizing the risks associated with such leveraged instruments.

feed.title: Lynn White, Chad Berkshire will be retiring from NCLH
feed.summary: Lynn White, EVP and chief people excellence officer, and Chad Berkshire, EVP and chief commercial officer, are set to retire from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings. White will depart by early January after 15 years, and Berkshire will leave in 2026 after nearly 18 years. Keith Wilmot has been promoted to EVP and chief people officer, succeeding White, while Berkshire's replacement is yet to be identified.

feed.title: Equinix Inc (EQIX) Insider Sell: Raouf Abdel Sells 403 Shares
feed.summary: Raouf Abdel, Executive Vice President, Global Operations at Equinix Inc, sold 403 shares of the company on December 10, 2025. This transaction brings his total holdings to 6,130.658 shares. Equinix Inc, trading at $740 per share with a market cap of $74,045.070 million, is considered modestly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.86, despite a high price-earnings ratio of 69.06.

feed.title: Texas Instruments, Intel Corporation microchips in Russian missiles, lawsuits say
feed.summary: Several lawsuits filed in Texas accuse Texas Instruments, Intel, Advanced Micro Devices, and Mouser Electronics of supplying microchips found in Russian missiles used in attacks against Ukrainian civilians. The lawsuits allege these companies evaded U.S. export laws and "knew or had reason to know" their components were being used in weapons, contributing to wrongful deaths. Intel and Mouser Electronics have stated they operate in accordance with export laws, with Intel having suspended business in Russia and Belarus, while Texas Instruments and AMD have not yet commented.

feed.title: FDA Approves Amgen's UPLIZNA for New Myasthenia Gravis Treatment
feed.summary: Amgen (AMGN) has received FDA approval for UPLIZNA (inebilizumab-cdon) to treat adults with generalized myasthenia gravis (gMG) who test positive for specific antibodies. This approval is based on data from the largest Phase 3 biologic study for gMG, offering a targeted therapy potentially requiring only two annual doses after an initial loading phase. While Amgen shows strong financial metrics, including robust profitability and revenue growth, investors should note warning signs like its Altman Z-Score and recent insider selling activity.

feed.title: Can Jabil (JBL) Turn New Cooling Investments Into a Durable High-Value Manufacturing Edge?
feed.summary: Analysts maintain positive views on Jabil (JBL) due to new investments in liquid cooling, a North Carolina factory, and the proposed Hanley Energy acquisition, which are expected to boost high-value manufacturing. While these developments reinforce Jabil's push into advanced solutions like AI infrastructure, they also introduce short-term risks such as capacity constraints and inventory pressures. The company aims for significant revenue growth, with the North Carolina facility projected to contribute $1 billion annually, but must navigate production headwinds.

feed.title: Weymouth Katharine buys Republic Services (RSG) stock worth $100,121
feed.summary: Katharine Weymouth, a director at Republic Services Inc (NYSE:RSG), purchased 478 shares of the company's common stock for $100,121. This transaction increased her direct ownership to 5740 shares. The purchase follows Republic Services exceeding Q3 2025 earnings expectations, though revenue slightly missed, and various analyst firms have provided mixed ratings and price targets.

feed.title: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (NASDAQ:AMUU) Raises Dividend to $6.70 Per Share
feed.summary: Direxion Daily AMD Bull 2X Shares (AMUU) announced a significant increase in its quarterly dividend to $6.7021 per share, representing a 2,716.1% increase from its previous payout, with a record date of December 10th and payable on December 17th. This dividend hike implies a 44.9% yield. Despite the substantial dividend increase, the stock experienced a 10.1% decline in trading on Wednesday, closing at $59.77.

feed.title: Teamsters’ Four‑Year Wage Deal Could Be A Game Changer For Sysco’s (SYY) Labor Cost Profile
feed.summary: Sysco's labor costs are set to increase after the Teamsters ratified a new four-year regional collective bargaining agreement for over 1,000 drivers and warehouse workers in Northern California and Nevada. This deal includes a 34% pay rise, higher pension contributions, and enhanced healthcare. While raising labor costs, it mitigates immediate strike risks, slightly reshaping Sysco's investment narrative focused on cost discipline and operational execution.

feed.title: Calvin McDonald, Lululemon CEO, Will Depart Yoga Clothing Maker in January
feed.summary: Calvin McDonald, CEO of Lululemon Athletica, will step down at the end of January 2026, following public criticism from company founder Chip Wilson regarding performance and lack of innovation. The news, coupled with strong third-quarter earnings, caused Lululemon's shares to jump 10 percent. The company is searching for a replacement, with interim co-CEOs Meghan Frank and André Maestrini taking over in the meantime.

feed.title: Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) gets FDA OK for Uplizna as first CD19 B-cell therapy for gMG
feed.summary: Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) announced FDA approval of UPLIZNA (inebilizumab-cdon) for the treatment of generalized myasthenia gravis (gMG) in adults who are anti-AChR and anti-MuSK antibody positive. This approval is based on data from the Phase 3 MINT trial, demonstrating significant symptom control and marking UPLIZNA as the first CD19-targeted B-cell therapy for these gMG subtypes, with a convenient twice-yearly maintenance dosing schedule. UPLIZNA already has two previous FDA approvals for NMOSD and IgG4-RD, establishing it as a multi-indication therapy.

feed.title: Closing Cyber Gaps: CrowdStrike + Absolute Resilience
feed.summary: This article announces a webinar featuring CrowdStrike and Absolute Security, focusing on building cyber resilience that extends beyond traditional prevention. It addresses the issue of "silent control failure" and how combining CrowdStrike's detection with Absolute's firmware-embedded resilience can keep security controls healthy and endpoints recoverable, even when the OS fails. The webinar aims to provide a framework for operational resilience and new metrics for CISOs.

feed.title: Commvault and Delinea partner to boost protection against credential-based attacks
feed.summary: Commvault Systems Inc. and Delinea Inc. have announced a new partnership to enhance protection against credential-based attacks. Their integration connects the Commvault Cloud platform with Delinea’s Secret Server, aiming to strengthen credential security, support compliance, and streamline data recovery for joint customers. This collaboration addresses the rising number of security incidents stemming from compromised privileged identities by centralizing credential management and introducing just-in-time access for backup environments.

feed.title: Dynagas LNG Partners Launches $10 Million Unit Repurchase Program
feed.summary: Dynagas LNG Partners has initiated a new $10 million common unit repurchase program, replacing a previous one that expired. The program allows for flexible repurchases through November 2026, aiming to impact the company’s market positioning and shareholder value. An analyst has rated DLNG stock a Buy with a $4.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as Outperform due to strong financial performance and attractive valuation.

feed.title: Adamas Trust declares quarterly dividend of $0.23 per share
feed.summary: Adamas Trust, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADAM) has announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.23 per share on its common stock, with a 12.78% current yield and 22 consecutive years of dividend payments. The company also declared cash dividends on its preferred stock series. This announcement follows a strong third quarter in 2025, with earnings per share reaching $0.24, marking the sixth consecutive quarterly increase.

feed.title: Epsilon Energy Ltd. Announces the Divestment of Dewey Energy Holdings LLC and the Company's Asset in the Anadarko Basin
feed.summary: Epsilon Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ: EPSN) announced the sale of its wholly-owned subsidiary, Dewey Energy Holdings, LLC, which includes its Western Anadarko basin assets, to an undisclosed private buyer. The sale, valued at $2.5 million in cash, divests approximately 813 Mcfe/d of production and 6,400 net deep acres and 2,200 net shallow acres of leasehold in Dewey County, Oklahoma. This strategic move allows Epsilon Energy to streamline its asset portfolio.

feed.title: Community Trust Bancorp (NASDAQ:CTBI) Hits New 52-Week High - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Community Trust Bancorp (NASDAQ:CTBI) recently hit a new 52-week high of $59.68, with analysts maintaining a "Buy" consensus rating and an average price target of $63.50. The financial services provider trades at a relatively value-oriented P/E of 11.5 and offers a 3.6% dividend yield. Despite missing Q3 EPS and revenue estimates, the company shows a strong net margin and return on equity, and institutional investors have significantly increased their positions.
